subject: Science
chapter: The Invisible Living World: Beyond Our Naked Eye
source_pdf: The Invisible Living World Beyond Our Naked Eye.pdf

activities_projects.title: Water Flask Magnification Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand how lenses and magnification help us see small objects
steps: Fill a round-bottom flask with water
Place it on an open book
Look at the letters through the flask
Observe how the letters appear larger
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the concept of magnification and the role of lenses
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation and explanation of magnification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Observation of Onion Peel Cells
activities_projects.objective: Observe plant cells using a microscope
steps: Take a thin onion peel from the inner layer
Place it in safranin stain for 30 seconds
Rinse with water and place on a slide
Add glycerin and cover with a coverslip
Observe under a microscope
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn the structure of plant cells
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of cell diagram and observations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Observation of Human Cheek Cells
activities_projects.objective: Understand the structure of animal cells
steps: Gently scrape the inside of the cheek using a toothpick
Spread the sample on a slide with water
Add methylene blue stain
Cover with a coverslip
Observe under the microscope
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify nucleus, cytoplasm, and cell membrane
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct observation and labeled diagram (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Observation of Pond Water Microorganisms
steps: Collect pond water in a container
Place a drop of water on a slide
Cover with a coverslip
Observe under a microscope
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about microorganisms like Amoeba and Paramecium
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification and drawings of microorganisms (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Soil Suspension Observation
steps: Mix soil with water to form soil suspension
Allow the mixture to settle
Take a drop of the top liquid
Observe under a microscope
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that soil contains many microorganisms

activities_projects.title: Compost Formation Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of microbes in decomposition
steps: Fill a container halfway with soil
Add fruit and vegetable peels
Cover with soil
Observe after 2–3 weeks
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how microorganisms convert waste into manure

activities_projects.title: Yeast and Dough Fermentation
steps: Prepare dough in two bowls
Add yeast to one bowl only
Cover both bowls with a damp cloth
Observe changes after 4–5 hours
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand fermentation and gas production by yeast

activities_projects.title: Curd Formation Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Study the role of bacteria in milk fermentation
steps: Add curd to warm milk in bowl A
Add curd to cold milk in bowl B
Leave both bowls for several hours
Observe which milk turns into curd
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of Lactobacillus bacteria

activities_projects.title: Bread Mould Growth
steps: Place one bread slice at room temperature
Place another in the refrigerator
Observe both slices after three days
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand environmental conditions affecting microbial growth

activities_projects.title: Study of Root Nodules in Legume Plants
steps: Carefully remove a legume plant from soil
Observe the roots
Identify swollen nodules on the roots
Discuss the role of Rhizobium bacteria
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about nitrogen fixation and soil fertility
